title: US Stock Market Today: S&P 500 Futures Slip As Rate Hike Worries Persist
content:
Get insights on thousands of stocks from the global community of over 7 million individual investors at Simply Wall St.

The Morning Bull - US Market Morning Update Friday, Jun, 5 2026

US stock futures are mixed this morning, with Dow contracts edging higher while S&P 500 and Nasdaq futures slip, as investors weigh strong US demand against stubborn inflation and higher rate expectations. The ISM Services index sits at 54.5, a reading that signals many service businesses are still busy, which tends to support jobs and spending. At the same time, durable goods orders are up 4.8%, showing solid appetite for big ticket items like aircraft. Yet the 10 year Treasury yield near 4.48% and market odds of a Federal Reserve hike around 85% keep pressure on growth stocks and rate sensitive sectors such as tech and utilities, and put more attention on companies tied to services and industrial spending.

Wall Street's queuing for one rocket. While SpaceX counts down to its IPO, other companies tied to the new space race are already in orbit. → 20 Compelling Space Companies watchlist · Global Space Race Investing Ideas screener · Scan the sector by valuation on Rocket Lab's valuation page.

Top Movers

Innio (INIO) surged 23.33% after completing a US$2.43b IPO of 90,000,000 common shares. Reddit (RDDT) gained 8.51% after Loop Capital reiterated a Buy rating and set a US$260 price target. Blackstone (BX) climbed 7.50% as investors focused on its role in Anthropic's US$36b debt financing.

Is Reddit still a smart investment or just hype? Read our most popular narrative and get all the answers you need.INIO 1-Year Stock Price Chart

Top Losers

Ciena (CIEN) fell 13.66% after earnings and guidance arrived following a strong year-to-date rally. Broadcom (AVGO) declined 12.59% as investors reacted to mixed AI expectations and a downgrade tied to Google chips. Micron Technology (MU) fell 7.74%.

For a broader view of stocks under pressure, line up potential opportunities and risks with the 47 high quality undervalued stocks

Look past the noise - uncover the top narrative that explains what truly matters for Ciena's long-term success.CIEN 1-Year Stock Price Chart

On The Radar

Macro data from Japan and Europe will share the spotlight with a handful of high profile US tech and internet company meetings.

US mega cap tech governance:Alphabet (GOOGL) AGM on Friday, focusing on board elections and oversight priorities. Global internet platforms:Airbnb (ABNB) AGM on Friday reviews executive pay, auditor ratification and shareholder proposals. Japan growth pulse:Japan Q1 GDP Growth Annualized Final on Sunday shapes views on regional demand and policy. Japan trade and income flows:Japan Current Account for April on Sunday highlights external balances and global trade conditions. Eurozone manufacturing signals:Germany Factory Orders for April on Monday helps frame demand for industrial and export exposed stocks.

Story Continues

title: Innio (INIO) Rides on Energy Optimism, Soars 23%
content:
Innio NV (NASDAQ:INIO) is one of the 9 Stocks Crushing the Market With Surging Returns.

Innio soared by 23.33 percent in its first day as a publicly-listed company, riding the strong investor optimism for energy stocks amid the continued growing demand.

In intra-day trading, Innio NV (NASDAQ:INIO) opened at $31, marking a jump of 14.8 percent from its initial public offering price of $27, climbed by as high as 27.6 percent to $34.45, before trimming gains to end the session at $33.30.

The New York Stock Exchange building. Photo by Дмитрий Трепольский on Pexels

Innio NV (NASDAQ:INIO) said that the IPO consisted of 90 million secondary shares, sold for $27 apiece for a total of $2.43 billion, and will be fully received by the selling shareholder. This means that Innio NV (NASDAQ:INIO) will not receive any amount from the proceeds.

Meanwhile, the company also granted its underwriters the right to purchase up to an additional 13.5 million shares from the selling shareholder at the IPO price, for a total of $364.5 million.

Backed by Advent and ADIA, Innio NV (NASDAQ:INIO) is a global distributed energy solutions provider that commits to delivering reliable, flexible, transient, decentralized, modular, and efficient power through the design, manufacture, and service of high-performance power systems under its Jenbacher and Waukesha brands.

The company delivers power for applications including data centers, microgrids, grid stabilization, industrial energy, and gas compression.

While we acknowledge the potential of INIO as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. title: Power Equipment Maker Innio Debuts Strongly on Nasdaq
content:
Shares of power generation equipment maker Innio jumped 15% in their Nasdaq debut after the company’s largest shareholders raised $2.43 billion in an upsized initial public offering, underscoring strong investor appetite for energy infrastructure companies linked to growing electricity demand.

The Munich-based company’s shares opened at $31, above the IPO price of $27, giving Innio a market valuation of roughly $23.7 billion. The offering consisted of 90 million shares sold by existing shareholders, including private equity firm Advent International and the Abu Dhabi Investment Authority (ADIA), up from the 75 million shares initially marketed.

Innio manufactures gas engines under its Jenbacher and Waukesha brands and provides power plant monitoring and optimization services through its MyPlant software platform. The company operates production facilities in Austria, Canada, and the United States and serves customers across the power generation and industrial sectors.

The IPO comes amid growing investor interest in companies expected to benefit from rising electricity demand driven by artificial intelligence, data centers, and grid modernization. Several industrial and power equipment firms have tapped public markets this year as investors seek exposure to the expanding energy infrastructure sector.

Innio reported first-quarter 2026 revenue of $668.6 million, up from $494 million a year earlier, although the company posted a net loss of $9 million compared with net income of $35 million in the prior-year period.

Advent acquired Innio, formerly part of General Electric’s distributed power business, for $3.25 billion in 2018. ADIA joined as an investor in 2023. Following the IPO, entities backed by Advent and ADIA will continue to control approximately 90% of the company.

The shares trade on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker symbol INIO.

title: Catalyst Watch: FedEx splits, tech CEOs hit Computex, Quantinuum IPO, and the jobs report
content:
[Wall Street New York stock exchange stock market]
alexsl

Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

MONDAY - JUNE 1

 	* VOLATILITY WATCH - Netflix (NFLX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NFLX]) and Globalstar (GSAT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GSAT]) have seen options volatility increase over the last week. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Momentus (MNTS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MNTS]), Hyliion (HYLN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HYLN]), and Redwire (RDW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RDW]). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Verra Mobility (VRRM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VRRM]) and Suja Life (SUJA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SUJA]). Short interest is elevated on Kohl's (KSS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KSS]) after its +20% earnings move. 
 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HPE]).
 	* DIVIDEND WATCH - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include McDonald's (MCD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MCD]), Halliburton (HAL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HAL]), Cigna (CI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CI]), and Western Digital (WDC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WDC]).
 	* IPO WATCH - Quantinuum (QNT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QNT]) headlines a busy week for IPOs. The Honeywell (HON [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HON])-backed quantum computing platform is looking to raise up to $1.05B and to value it at roughly $12.7B. Other IPOs expected to price and begin trading include aerospace components manufacturer Applied Aerospace & Defense (AADX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AADX]), silver miner Sunshine Silver Mining & Refining (GATO), Quantinuum (QNT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QNT]), coastal property insurer Safepoint Holdings (SFPT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SFPT]), and gas energy firm Innio Holding (INIO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/INIO]). The analyst quiet period ends on Hawkeye 360 (HAWK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HAWK]), Suja Life (SUJA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SUJA]), Rare Earths Americas (REA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/REA]), and Odyssey Therapeutics (ODTX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ODTX]) to free up analysts to post ratings.
 	* DATA WATCH - Key data reports due to arrive during the first few days of the week include updates on monthly deliveries from Nio (NIO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NIO]), XPeng (XPEV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XPEV]), Li Auto (LI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LI]), and BYD Company (BYDDF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BYDDF]). Other reports of high interest to investors will be the monthly Macau gross gaming revenue release, the monthly update on Class 8 truck orders, firearm background check data from the FBI, and discount broker DARTs reports.
 	* ALL DAY - FedEx (FDX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FDX]) will officially split off its less-than-truckload trucking arm, FedEx Freight (FDXF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FDXF]), into a separate, publicly traded company while keeping the express and ground parcel businesses inside FedEx Corp. The move is structured as a tax-efficient spin-off. FedEx Freight will be a component of the S&P 500 Index right out of the gate.
 	* ALL DAY - The four-day Computex Taipei event will begin. The AI-first show includes a wall-to-wall lineup of chip CEOs, heavyweight forums on physical AI, and a dense calendar of launches spanning PCs, data centers, edge, and robotics. Qualcomm (QCOM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QCOM]) CEO Cristiano Amon, Intel (INTC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/INTC]) CEO Lip-Bu Tan, Arm (ARM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ARM]) CEO Rene Haas, and Marvell Technologies (MRVL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MRVL]) CEO Matt Murphy will all give keynotes or major presentations. Nvidia is effectively co-branding its Computex presence as "NVIDIA GTC Taipei at COMPUTEX," running across the week of the show and CEO Jensen Huang delivers a high-profile keynote. Cisco (CSCO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CSCO]) also plans a big presence.
 	* ALL DAY - Snowflake Summit 26 next week will begin in San Francisco. Snowflake's (SNOW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SNOW]) four-day flagship conference in San Francisco will be focused on "Making AI Real for Business," with heavy emphasis on enterprise data, generative/agentic AI, and applications.
 	* ALL DAY - The Atlantic hurricane season officially begins. Traders expect more volatility than normal around crude oil futures this year if any major hurricanes develop near offshore drilling assets.

TUESDAY - JUNE 2

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Palo Alto Networks (PANW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PANW]), Dollar General (DG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DG]), Ulta Beauty (ULTA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ULTA]), Signet Jewelers (SIG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SIG]), and Yesway (YSWY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/YSWY]), which will be reporting for the first time as a public company. Options trading implies double-digit swings for Oddity Tech (ODD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ODD]) and Victoria's Secret (VSCO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VSCO]) after their reports are released.
 	* ALL DAY - Federal Reserve Bank of Minneapolis President Neel Kashkari is scheduled to participate in a panel discussion at the 2026 Bank of Korea International Conference.
 	* ALL DAY - The Microsoft Build event will begin in San Francisco. The flagship developer showcase will kick off with a keynote from Microsoft (MSFT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MSFT]) CEO Satya Nadella.
 	* ALL DAY - The two-day Deutsche Bank Basic Materials Conference will begin. The conference will include a long list of presentations from senior executives across chemicals, mining, industrials, and related basic materials companies. 
 	* 8:30 A.M. Cleveland Federal Reserve Bank President Beth Hammack will give remarks and participate in a conversation on monetary policy.

WEDNESDAY - JUNE 3

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Broadcom (AVGO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AVGO]), Crowdstrike (CRWD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRWD]), Five Below (FIVE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FIVE]), Medtronic (MDT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MDT]), and Macy's (M [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/M]). Options trading implies double-digit swings for Netskope (NTSK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NTSK]) and Petco Health and Wellness (WOOF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WOOF]) after their reports are released.
 	* ALL DAY - Honeywell (HON [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HON]) will hold an Aerospace Investor Day event ahead of the planned spinoff of Honeywell Aerospace later in the year.
 	* 3:00 P.M. The Federal Reserve will release its Beige Book Report. The report will summarize current economic conditions across the Fed's districts.
 	* POSTMARKET - Costco (COST [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/COST]) will release its May sales report.

THURSDAY - JUNE 4

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Ciena (CIEN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CIEN]), Samsara (IOT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IOT]), Rubrik (RBRK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RBRK]), Docusign (DOCU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DOCU]), and Lululemon Athletica (LULU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LULU]). Options trading implies a double-digit swing for X-Energy (XE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XE]) and Planet Labs (PL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PL]) after they report.
 	* ALL DAY - Notable investor events include Radian Group's (RDN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RDN]) Investor Day, Solstice Advanced Materials' (SOLS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SOLS]) business update call, and Lucid Group's (LCID [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LCID]) annual meeting.
 	* ALL DAY - Boralex (BRLXF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BRLXF]) (BLX:CA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BLX:CA]) shareholders will vote on the acquisition offer from Brookfield Asset Management (BAM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BAM]) (BEP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BEP]) (BN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BN]) and Caisse de Depot et Placement du Quebec.

FRIDAY - JUNE 5

 	* ALL DAY - The Manheim Used Vehicle Value Index will be released.
 	* ALL DAY - It is the one-year anniversary date of the Circle (CRCL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRCL]) IPO.
 	* ALL DAY - The live-action _Masters of the Universe_ movie from Amazon MGM Studios and Mattel Studios is set to hit U.S. theaters. Mattel (MAT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MAT]) has a significant merchandising program tied to the film.
 	* ALL DAY - The Summer Game Fest conference will take place. The video game conference could generate buzz that is relevant to Sony's (SONY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SONY]) PlayStation, Nintendo (NTDOY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NTDOY]), Microsoft's (MSFT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MSFT]) Xbox, and major third-party publishers like Capcom, Square Enix, Bandai Namco, CD Projekt, and Epic Games.
 	* 8:30 A.M. The May jobs report will be released. Economists expect 93K job additions for the month and for the unemployment rate to remain at 4.3%.

_Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this article._

title: INNIO Group launches roadmap for $2 billion Nasdaq IPO
content:
Investing.com -- INNIO Group, a distributed energy solutions provider, has launched its roadshow for an initial public offering of 75 million common shares offered by a selling shareholder.

The company estimates the IPO price between $24.00 and $27.00 per share. INNIO will raise up to $2.03 billion at the top of the range pricing. The selling shareholder has granted underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 11.25 million common shares at the IPO price.

INNIO has applied to list its common shares on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker symbol "INIO."

Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC, J.P. Morgan, and Morgan Stanley serve as joint lead book-running managers for the offering. BofA Securities, Barclays, and Citigroup act as book-running managers, while Baird, BNP Paribas, Deutsche Bank Securities, RBC Capital Markets, and UBS Investment Bank function as bookrunners.

The company, backed by private equity firm Advent and Abu Dhabi Investment Authority, reported a net loss of $7.2 million on revenue of $668.6 million in the first three months of 2026 compared to net income of $35 million on revenue of $494 million in the same period the year before, according to its filing with the US Securities and Exchange Commission.

Munich-based INNIO sells engines under the brands Jenbacher and Waukesha, and offers an AI-powered software platform for power plants called Myplant. The company has production hubs in Austria, Canada and the US.

The company's five largest customers accounted for about 39% of its revenue in the first quarter of 2026.

title: INNIO Group Announces Filing of Registration Statement for Proposed Initial Public Offering
content:
INNIO Group Announces Filing of Registration Statement for Proposed Initial Public Offering

MUNICH, May 11, 2026--(BUSINESS WIRE)--INNIO Group ("INNIO"), a leading global distributed energy solutions provider, today announces that it has publicly filed a registration statement on Form S-1 with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission ("SEC") relating to a proposed initial public offering of its common shares.

The timing of the offering, number of shares to be offered and the price range for the proposed offering have not yet been determined. INNIO has applied to list its common shares on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker symbol "INIO."

Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC, J.P. Morgan and Morgan Stanley are acting as joint lead book-running managers for the proposed offering. BofA Securities, Barclays and Citigroup are acting as book-running managers for the proposed offering. Baird, BNP Paribas, Deutsche Bank Securities, RBC Capital Markets and UBS Investment Bank are acting as bookrunners for the proposed offering. Credit Agricole CIB, Erste Group, UniCredit, Academy Securities and Drexel Hamilton are acting as co-managers for the proposed offering.

The proposed offering is subject to market and other conditions, including effectiveness of such registration statement, and there can be no assurance as to whether or when the offering may be commenced or completed, or as to the actual size, price or other terms of the proposed offering.

The proposed offering will be made only by means of a prospectus. Copies of the preliminary prospectus related to the proposed offering, when available, may be obtained from:

Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC, Attention: Prospectus Department, 200 West Street, New York, NY 10282, by telephone at (866) 471-2526, or by email at prospectus-ny@ny.email.gs.com; J.P. Morgan Securities LLC, c/o Broadridge Financial Solutions, 1155 Long Island Avenue, Edgewood, NY 11717, or by email at prospectus-eq_fi@jpmchase.com and postsalemanualrequests@broadridge.com; or Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC, Attention: Prospectus Department, 180 Varick Street, 2nd Floor, New York, NY 10014.

A registration statement relating to these securities has been filed with the SEC but has not yet become effective. These securities may not be sold, nor may offers to buy these securities be accepted, prior to the time the registration statement becomes effective. This press release does not constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy any securities, and shall not constitute an offer, solicitation or sale in any jurisdiction in which such offer, solicitation or sale would be unlawful prior to registration or qualification under the securities laws of that jurisdiction.

Story Continues

About INNIO Group

INNIO Group is a global distributed energy solutions provider that delivers reliable, flexible, transient, decentralized, modular and efficient power. With a track record of innovation, INNIO designs, manufactures and services high‑performance power systems under its Jenbacher and Waukesha brands. The company delivers power for applications including data centers, microgrids, grid stabilization, industrial energy and gas compression.

INNIO operates a global installed base across approximately 100 countries as of December 31, 2025, supported by a resilient, high‑margin services business that delivers long‑term, recurring revenues across the full equipment lifecycle. As electricity demand accelerates—driven by AI, electrification and grid constraints—INNIO enables scalable, behind‑the‑meter power generation with high efficiency, fast start capability, strong transient performance and fuel flexibility, including hydrogen‑ready solutions. Headquartered in Munich, Germany, INNIO employs over 5,000 people worldwide and is committed to moving energy forward.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260511389675/en/
